title: Bootstrap admin: first caller auto-granted admin, subsequent grants require existing admin

requirements.text: Add a one-time admin bootstrap mechanism to the backend: if `adminPrincipal` has never been set (i.e., it holds a sentinel/anonymous value from initial deployment), the first authenticated caller of a new `claimAdmin` update function is automatically and permanently granted the `adminPrincipal` role. Subsequent calls to `claimAdmin` must be rejected once an admin is already set. All existing admin-only functions continue to require the caller to match the stored `adminPrincipal`.

quotes: The first account to ever log in should be made admin - but none afterwards, unless defined by existing admin.

acceptanceCriteria: On a fresh deployment with no admin set, calling `claimAdmin` with an authenticated (non-anonymous) principal stores that principal as `adminPrincipal` and returns a success result.
A second call to `claimAdmin` from any principal returns an error and does not change `adminPrincipal`.
After bootstrap, the existing `updateAdmin` function (callable only by the current admin) remains the sole way to transfer or change the admin principal.
All previously gated admin functions (add/update/delete listing, upload credential, hide/delete vouch, etc.) continue to enforce that the caller matches `adminPrincipal`.
Anonymous callers are rejected by `claimAdmin` and receive an error.

requirements.text: Expose a `claimAdmin` call from the frontend Admin Dashboard page: if the authenticated user is not currently admin and no admin has been set yet (backend returns a designated 'unclaimed' status), display a prominent 'Claim Admin' button. On click, call the `claimAdmin` backend function and refresh the admin status check. If an admin is already set and the current user is not that admin, continue showing the existing 'Access Denied' message.

quotes: The first account to ever log in should be made admin - but none afterwards, unless defined by existing admin.
Please resolve so that I can test all functionality.

acceptanceCriteria: When the backend `adminPrincipal` is unset/unclaimed, the Admin Dashboard shows a 'Claim Admin' button instead of 'Access Denied'.
Clicking 'Claim Admin' invokes the `claimAdmin` backend function and, on success, refreshes the page/admin status so the full dashboard is displayed.
If `claimAdmin` fails (admin already claimed by another principal), an error toast is shown and the 'Access Denied' message is rendered.
Once the current user is confirmed as admin, the full dashboard renders normally without requiring a page reload beyond the query invalidation.

constraints: Do not change existing admin-gated function signatures or access control checks â€” only add the bootstrap `claimAdmin` path.
The bootstrap claim must be a one-shot operation: once `adminPrincipal` is set via `claimAdmin`, the function must permanently reject further claims.
Anonymous principals must never be granted admin via any path.
nonGoals: Multi-admin or role-based access control â€” a single adminPrincipal remains the model.
Resetting or revoking admin via `claimAdmin` â€” only the existing `updateAdmin` flow handles transfers.
Any changes to purchase, credential, or vouch logic unrelated to the admin bootstrap.
